sidebar.title: Connections
sidebar.subtitle: Manage RabbitMQ brokers and inspect topology
sidebar.emptyHint: Click “Add connection” to define an AMQP broker.
sidebar.deleteConfirmDetail:
Remove "{{name}}" ({{host}}) from saved connections?

This cannot be undone.
sidebar.exportWarn: Passwords are stored in plaintext in this file — handle with care.

about.title: About
about.description: MQ Browser — a Tauri + React desktop client for RabbitMQ. Topology, messages, policies, admin and routing analysis through the management HTTP API.

dialog.amqpPortHint: Metadata only — this app communicates with the broker via the management HTTP API, not AMQP.
dialog.mgmtPortHint: RabbitMQ management plugin port. 15672 plaintext / 15671 TLS. Required.
dialog.labelTlsHint: Enable when the management URL starts with https://
dialog.testHint: Test calls /api/overview against the management plugin.

empty.title: Pick a connection
empty.hint: Select a broker on the left to explore its exchanges, queues, bindings, and messages.

queues.purgeConfirmDetail: Drop every message in "{{name}}". This cannot be undone.
queues.deleteConfirmDetail: Delete queue "{{name}}" from the broker. This cannot be undone.

empty.title: No queues in this vhost yet
empty.hint: Queues hold messages until consumers acknowledge them. Create one to start receiving traffic from producers.

hint.ready: Messages waiting to be delivered to a consumer.
hint.unacked: Delivered to a consumer but not yet acknowledged.
hint.total: ready + unacked. The full backlog held by the broker.

empty.title: No exchanges in this vhost
empty.hint: Exchanges route messages to queues based on routing rules (direct, fanout, topic, headers).

exchanges.deleteConfirmDetail: Delete exchange "{{name}}". Bindings on it will also be removed.

empty.title: No bindings configured
empty.hint: Bindings connect exchanges to queues (or other exchanges) using a routing key.

messages.title: Peek messages in "{{name}}"
messages.requeueOffWarning: Requeue is OFF: fetched messages are dropped (sent to DLX if configured, otherwise lost).

definitions.title: Broker definitions
definitions.help: Export saves the full broker definitions (queues, exchanges, bindings, policies, users) as JSON. Import overlays the same JSON onto the broker — existing objects are merged, missing ones are created.

target.queueHint: Send directly to a queue (via default exchange).
target.exchangeHint: Send to an exchange with a routing key.

publish.targetQueueHint: Routed through the default exchange — routing key is the queue name.
publish.routingKeyPlaceholder: queue name when using default exchange
publish.success: Published. Broker routed it to at least one queue.
publish.unrouted: Published, but no queue matched the routing key (message dropped).
publish.hint: Sent through the management HTTP API. Failures surface as errors here.

connections.filterPlaceholder: Filter by name, user, peer host
connections.closeConfirmDetail: Force-close connection "{{name}}". The client will see a connection drop.

binding.title: Create binding
binding.editNote: RabbitMQ has no in-place binding edit. Saving will delete the existing binding and create a new one — there is a tiny window where the binding doesn't exist.

users.filterPlaceholder: Filter users by name or tag
users.deleteConfirmDetail: Delete user "{{name}}". All their permissions will also be revoked.

permissions.regexHint: Regex: ".*" = all, empty = none, anything else = matching object names.

vhosts.deleteConfirmDetail: Delete vhost "{{name}}". All queues/exchanges/bindings under it are removed.

rkAdvice.direct: Direct: messages route to bindings whose key matches exactly.
rkAdvice.topicBinding: Topic binding: * matches exactly one dot-separated segment, # matches zero or more segments. e.g. orders.*.us matches orders.created.us; logs.# matches logs, logs.error, logs.db.slow.
rkAdvice.topicBindingPlaceholder: wildcard pattern, e.g. orders.*.us or logs.#
rkAdvice.topicPublish: Topic publish: use a literal routing key (no * or #); the broker matches it against each binding's pattern segment by segment.
rkAdvice.fanout: Fanout broadcasts to all bindings — routing key is ignored.
rkAdvice.headers: Headers exchange matches on message headers, not routing key.
rkAdvice.unknown: Custom or unknown exchange type — routing key behavior depends on the broker plugin.

drill.notFound: "{{name}}" was not found in the current dataset (it may have been deleted or live in another vhost).

intro.connections: TCP connections from AMQP clients to the broker: who is connected, from which IP, protocol version, idle time. Start here when debugging "can't connect" or connection leaks.
intro.channels: Logical channels multiplexed inside each AMQP connection — publish, consume, and ack all run on channels. Watch unacked counts, prefetch, and whether flow control is engaged.
intro.consumers: Currently registered subscriptions — entries created by basic.consume that haven't been cancelled or disconnected. Shows the subscribed queue, prefetch, ack mode (manual/auto) and active status. Pull-style basic.get clients do not appear here.
intro.nodes: Per-node runtime health for the broker cluster: memory, file descriptors, sockets, free disk, Erlang processes. Hitting the memory high-watermark or disk_free_limit triggers flow control and blocks publishers.
intro.routingTester: Simulate a message: given an exchange + routing key + headers, compute which queues it would land in under the current binding topology. Useful for validating routing before publishing — no real message is produced.

host.noScheme: Host should not include http(s):// — put the scheme elsewhere

routing.intro: Simulate a publish: pick a source exchange and routing key — we trace bindings using direct/fanout/topic rules and show every queue (and intermediate exchange) the message would land in. Headers exchanges and x-* custom types are marked as 'potentially matches' (we can't infer message headers).
routing.noMatch: No queues or exchanges would receive a message with this key.
routing.fillIn: Pick a source exchange to start.

channels.closeConfirmDetail: Force-close channel "{{name}}". The client will see it disappear.

usage.title: Getting started
usage.step1: Click “Add connection”, fill in host / port / credentials. Defaults match a vanilla Docker `rabbitmq:management` image (guest/guest, ports 5672/15672).
usage.step2: Use “Test connection” — it calls /api/overview via the management plugin (port 15672) to confirm credentials and that the management plugin is enabled.
usage.step3: Switch vhost on the top bar to scope topology lookups; the “(all)” option queries the unfiltered endpoints.
usage.step4: Peek pulls messages with basic.get + reject(requeue=true); your broker copy is unchanged. Turn requeue OFF if you intentionally want to drain.
usage.step5: Publish uses lapin with publisher confirms. The default AMQP exchange routes by queue name in the routing key.
usage.note: lapin handles AMQP framing; topology + purge/delete go through the RabbitMQ Management HTTP API.
